title: Position: Breaking the Dual Curse of Multilingual AI Requires Socio-Technical Guardrails, Not Post-Hoc Alignment
venueLong: International Conference on Machine Learning — Position Paper Track
abstract: Multilingual language models exhibit a dual curse — harmful content generation rises to 35% in low-resource languages while instruction-following collapses. Reward models achieve only 49–50% accuracy in low-resource languages, undermining post-deployment safety. We argue for socio-technical guardrails built in from pre-training.

title: DIA-HARM: Dialectal Disparities in Harmful Content Detection Across 50 English Dialects
venueLong: 64th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
abstract: The first benchmark for evaluating disinformation detection robustness across 50 English dialects. We introduce the D3 corpus (195K samples) and evaluate 16 detection models, revealing systematic disparities across dialect groups.

title: BLUFF: Benchmarking in Low-resoUrce Languages for detecting Falsehoods and Fake news
venueLong: 32nd ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining — Datasets and Benchmarks Track
abstract: The largest multilingual fake news detection benchmark to date — 79 languages, 202K+ samples. Introduces AXL-CoI for adversarial cross-lingual generation and mPURIFY for quality filtering.
